game_name: The Magical Quest Starring Mickey Mouse

danger_signals.visual_form: Animated hostile creatures positioned in player path
danger_signals.meaning: Direct threat requiring evasion or combat

danger_signals.visual_form: Fires, obstacles, cliffs, and traps in level design
danger_signals.meaning: Spatial danger zones that damage or kill on contact

danger_signals.visual_form: Emperor Pete's domain with distinct visual theming
danger_signals.meaning: Peak danger threshold; final confrontation location

danger_signals.visual_form: Expandable health meter that decreases visually
danger_signals.meaning: Cumulative damage state; proximity to failure

safety_signals.meaning: Health recovery and meter expansion opportunity
Resource trading points; temporary safe haven

safety_signals.visual_form: Special equipment appearing in level progression
safety_signals.meaning: Power-up and ability enhancement; strategic advantage

safety_signals.visual_form: Goofy and other Disney characters in narrative moments
safety_signals.meaning: Allied presence; story progression and outfit gifting

faction_indicators.color_identity: Red and black traditional outfit as baseline
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Classic Mickey silhouette and cheerful demeanor
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Starting position; rescue mission objective framing

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Antagonist ruler of magical land; dognapper identity
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Positioned as final boss encounter with distinct domain
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Fourth level and beyond; Pete's domain represents faction territory
Narrative framing; Goofy gifting mountain-climbing gear signals ally support

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Whimsical level theming with fantasy elements (magical land with distinct zones)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemy and NPC behavior follows magical logic; transformations possible via outfit system

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Park setting in opening narrative; bedroom in conclusion
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Casual catch game; mundane interaction before magical incursion

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Distinct visual environments per level (fire-themed, climbing terrain, etc.)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: New outfit availability corresponds to environmental challenge type

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Mickey's costume changes visually (magician, firefighter, climber gear)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Ability set shifts; water spraying, magic blasts, grappling hook mechanics activate

environmental_storytelling.example: Firefighter uniform acquired in fire-themed level 3; water mechanics solve fire hazards, demonstrating world logic
Mountain-climbing gear gifted by Goofy in level 4 for vertical traversal challenges, signaling ally support and level design intent
Shops scattered throughout levels telegraph safe resource exchange zones; coins as narrative currency of progress
Dream revelation ending juxtaposes magical land visuals against bedroom reality, establishing world-state ambiguity
Pluto's dognapping by Emperor Pete establishes stakes; reaching Pete's domain signals narrative climax proximity

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Basic platforming; grab and throw block combat; standard movement speed
Charge and release magic blasts; underwater breathing; magic carpet control; magic meter management replaces standard attacks
Spray water streams; extinguish fires; create icicles from water; solve fire-based environmental puzzles; water meter management
Deploy grappling hook for vertical movement and object interaction; swing and hoist mechanics; grab-and-throw combat variant
Patrol or attack patterns in designated zones; hazardous on contact; defeatable by jumping or thrown blocks
Multi-phase combat with distinct attack patterns; indicates final world-state (confrontation threshold)
Provides mountain-climbing gear; demonstrates ally faction support without direct combat participation

notes: The Magical Quest employs outfit-driven progression as its primary world-state manifestation mechanism. Visual communication heavily relies on costume changes to signal both player capability shifts and environmental thematic alignment. The dream framing creates narrative ambiguity about the magical land's reality status. Safety signals are primarily resource-based (hearts, shops) rather than environmental design (few 'safe zones' in traditional sense). Danger is conveyed through enemy presence, hazards, and health meter visibility. Factions are minimal; Pete represents clear antagonism while other Disney characters signal alliance through presence and item provision. Environmental storytelling is subordinate to mechanical progression.
